vBRIEFInfo.description: Project definition -- synthesized gestalt of the project.

narratives.Overview: Build the Phase 1 feature detail experience for deftConsensus with a two-pane brief/chat workflow, immutable original brief retention, governed AI-assisted in-progress updates, section-level voting, and parent/subfeature decomposition controls, all powered by centralized mock data.
narratives.Architecture: Use a nested Next.js App Router route (`companies/[companyId]/projects/[projectId]/features/[featureId]`) within the authenticated shell. The page composes a brief workspace (Original + In Progress), threaded chat surface, voting actions, and fork suggestions, backed by typed mock contracts for brief versions, votes, governance settings, and subfeature relationships. Governance settings resolve as company defaults with project overrides.
narratives.Requirements: FR-1: Feature detail route MUST be `companies/[companyId]/projects/[projectId]/features/[featureId]`. FR-2: Feature page MUST provide back navigation to project dashboard and visible hierarchy context in the header. FR-3: Desktop layout MUST use two panes (Brief left, Chat right); smaller breakpoints MUST use an accessible stacked/tabbed pattern. FR-4: Chat header MUST show participant avatars, compact after three users, and provide participant list + invite affordance. FR-5: Chat messages MUST render others left/current user right and support threaded replies. FR-6: Brief workspace MUST expose exactly two views: `Original` and `In Progress`. FR-7: Original brief MUST be immutable and persist as baseline snapshot. FR-8: In-progress brief MUST show AI provenance markers for updated sections and update timestamps. FR-9: AI updates MUST follow hybrid governance: low-risk wording/clarity edits auto-apply, structural or scope-changing edits require explicit approval. FR-10: Chat composer MUST include a Propose Vote shortcut that captures concise brief-change completion requests. FR-11: Voting MUST support section-level targets. FR-12: Voting model and thresholds MUST be configurable in settings with company-level defaults and project-level overrides. FR-13: Parent features MUST support decomposition into subfeatures; split should be system-suggested at complexity thresholds and require human confirmation. FR-14: Parent feature completion MUST require all required subfeatures complete; optional subfeatures may remain open. FR-15: System MUST auto-suggest forked brief requests for out-of-scope content, requiring user confirmation to create the fork. FR-16: Feature page MUST include explicit default/loading/empty/error states for both panes and key interactions. FR-17: All content/state in Phase 1 MUST use centralized mock data contracts only. NFR-1: Meet WCAG 2.1 AA baseline for keyboard navigation, focus visibility, landmarks, and contrast. NFR-2: Ensure responsive behavior across mobile/tablet/desktop with preserved collaboration usability. NFR-3: Use semantic design tokens and avoid hard-coded visual values without explicit justification. NFR-4: Add automated tests for route context, governance gating, section-level voting, subfeature logic, and fork suggestion flows.
narratives.ProjectConfig:
# deftConsensus Project Guidelines

Legend (from RFC2119): !=MUST, ~=SHOULD, ≉=SHOULD NOT, ⊗=MUST NOT, ?=MAY.

**deft_version**: 0.15.0

Only specify items here that **override or extend** the deft defaults.

**Reference**: `deft/main.md` | [languages/typescript.md](deft/languages/typescript.md)

## Project Configuration

**Tech Stack**: Web App using TypeScript with React and Next.js

## Strategy

Use [discuss](deft/strategies/discuss.md) for this project.

## Workflow

```bash
task check         # Pre-commit gate
task test:coverage # Coverage target
task build         # Build project
task clean         # Clean artifacts
```

## Standards

**Quality:**
- ! Run `task check` before every commit
- ! Achieve ≥85% coverage overall + per-module
- ! Store secrets in `secrets/` dir
- ~ Provide `.example` templates for secrets

## Project-Specific Rules

- ! Specification artifacts must be stored under specs/ using file-type subfolders:
  - SPECIFICATION.md files in specs/specifications/
  - PRD.md files in specs/prds/
  - discussion/context outputs (*-context.md) in specs/discussions/
- ! If a Deft strategy generates these files at the project root by default, move them into the required subfolder immediately and continue working from the moved path.
- ! Temporary policy: allow local-only git operations (`status`, `diff`, `add`, `commit`, `branch`, `log`); ⊗ do not perform remote git operations (`push`, `pull`, `fetch`, remote rebase/merge) unless explicitly revoked by the user.
- ! During Phase 1, frontend scope is isolated: ⊗ no live integrations (no APIs, servers, databases, auth providers, or external services); use mock data only; keep all mock data centralized.
- ! Before frontend feature implementation, draft and obtain user approval on a comprehensive brand guide covering color system, typography, spacing, component style principles, tone/voice, and accessibility baseline.
- ! Use centralized semantic design tokens by default for frontend styling; hard-coded visual values in feature code require explicit justification.
- ! Before implementing any Phase 1 route/page, complete and obtain user sign-off on page specs including route path/name, page goal and primary action, required sections/components, navigation entry/exit, UI states (default/loading/empty/error), required mock data shape, responsive behavior, and accessibility expectations.

---

**Generated by**: deft-setup skill
**Date**: 2026-04-11
